---
title: "Data Projects with R and GitHub: Movies and Inflation"
output: md_document
---

*In order to make the first feedback loop as efficient as possible, I sticked with short phrases and keypoints so far. I will later on add more details and explanations to the project description to make it more similar to Martin's project.*

*The given dataset can be downloaded [here](https://zenodo.org/records/5653247).*

The dataset provides numerous details about movies which include: 

- Title: movie title.
- Release_Year: year of the movie's release.
- Watchtime: movie duration in minutes.
- Genre: genre.
- Movie_Rating: IMDB movie rating.
- Metascore: metacritic movie rating.
- Votes: number of ratings.
- Gross_collection: incomes generated by the movie.
- Sinopsis.
- Director.
- Star: main actors.
- Gross_equivalent: income generated by movies with dollar devaluation correction

## Tasks for Data clean up:

- fix first column so that every film is assigned with one specific number
- remove special characters from variable name
- round the numeric values to the closest full number to eliminate all decimals
- create an object where you store all films that have no NAs at all (will be needed later on for the visualization part)

## Tasks for Data manipulation:

Of course, the first question that comes to mind is: What is the overall best movie? Find the movie that has got the highest score when combining Movie_Rating (you have renamed it!) and Metascore.

Another relevant measurement is, how much money films made through the sale of tickets. However, many films are pretty old so we want to learn more about inflation throughout allmthe years. For each year: Calculate the rate of inflation by using the given data of the Gross_collection variable
  and Gross_equivalent variable and store it in a new variable.

Some Advice:
- Use data without NAs for the calculations to make sure that you have a complete dataset for the manipulation part.
- Group data by year
- calculate sum of Gross_collection for each year
- calculate sum of Gross_equivalent for each year
- use simple mathematics each individual rate of inflation 
(try to write the code as efficient as possible!)

## Tasks for Data visualization:

Let's see if there is a correlation between Movie_Score and Metascore. To do so, create a scatterplot with Movie_Score on the x-axis and Metascore on the y-axis. Here you can use the data frame with no NAs to make sure that you have a complete dataset for the visualization. This should eventually look like this: 
![excample_scatterplots](https://dr-eberle-zentrum.github.io/Data-projects-with-R-and-GitHub/Projects/Jannis-DW/Scatter_plot_422d3c3f82.png)

If you would like to even improve that: Mark all the films that your favourite actor/actress has participated in with a special color to see how they performed!

And if you still could not get enough: Adjust the size of each point within the scatterplot to the number of votes! This way we can see hoe many people have rated the movies and how that correlates with the movie score and the metacritic score.
